abstract: Tabular data, structured in rows and columns, serves as the backbone of empirical research across domains as diverse as particle physics, biomedicine, and economics. Missing value prediction—specifically, inferring a target label from a set of features—is a fundamental task in these fields, underpinning everything from drug discovery to climate modeling. While deep learning has transformed our ability to process raw data such as images and text, gradient-boosted decision trees have remained the undisputed gold standard for tabular data for two decades. Here, we introduce the Tabular Prior-data Fitted Network (TabPFN), a tabular foundation model that shifts this paradigm. TabPFN outperforms existing methods by a wide margin on datasets with up to 10,000 samples, while requiring orders of magnitude less training time. In just 2.8 seconds, TabPFN surpasses the performance of an ensemble of state-of-the-art baselines tuned for four hours. As a generative transformer-based model, it supports a wide array of downstream tasks, including fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, density estimation, and the creation of reusable embeddings. Developed by training a meta-learning algorithm across millions of synthetic datasets, TabPFN demonstrates the transformative potential of learned algorithms. By significantly advancing modeling capabilities, TabPFN offers a powerful tool to accelerate scientific discovery and refine critical decision-making processes across the sciences.
